human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for technical-documentation specificity, records-documentation-and-administration coverage, professional-services confidentiality and approval boundaries, research source quality, functionality-map completeness, evaluation consistency, deployment posture, and validated spec-pack readiness.

notes: Research summary includes authoritative public sources with publisher, source class, authority rationale, workflow-stage usage, practical use, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy.
Source mix covers documentation workflow, open standards, API terminology, normative requirements language, accessibility, security evidence, confidentiality, and recordkeeping.
All shared abilities, all records-documentation-and-administration workflow abilities, and both professional-and-information-services abilities are represented and validated.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in functionality scenario coverage.

Scenario coverage explicitly includes normal implementation/design documentation, maintainability and correctness review, security and quality expectations, missing evidence, conflicting records, unauthorized record changes, confidentiality conflicts, low-confidence escalation, and adjacent-specialist refusals.

Output fidelity is covered for Markdown-first documentation plans and edits, structured review reports, gap analyses, evidence packets, record update notes, audit-trail summaries, and escalation notes.

Boundary rules explicitly reject platform operations, IAM, networking, patch deployment, code implementation, legal or licensing work, compliance certification, release engineering ownership, production incident response, product strategy, pricing pages, GTM collateral, and final approval claims.
Tenant adaptation is limited to configuration, retrieval, approved tuning, and tenant style overlays that do not weaken source traceability, confidentiality, approval, or refusal gates.
Structured readiness evidence supports a validated spec-pack claim.
Runtime may remain specification-based unless frequent-use promotion evidence justifies a retained implementation.
No long-term storage of a generated implementation is justified by this readiness record.
